extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. Transition t16 (on send_attempt if opted_out and stage>=4: sent := true) explicitly sets sent true in a state where opted_out is true, so the only question is whether a state with opted_out=true and stage==4 is reachable. It is. Trace from initial (opted_out=false, sent=false, stage=0): (1) key_0 [t3] -> stage=1; (2) key_0 [t6] -> stage=2; (3) key_2 [t9] -> stage=3; (4) key_0 [t12] -> stage=4 (now stage=4, opted_out=false, sent=false); (5) optout [t1] -> opted_out=true, sent=false, stage unchanged=4; (6) send_attempt -> t2 disabled (not opted_out is false), t15 disabled (stage<4 false), t16 enabled (opted_out and stage>=4) -> sent=true. Resulting state opted_out=true, sent=true violates the invariant. The 6-event sequence is within the 7-event bound. The stage-climb resets sent to false at each step but never clears opted_out, and optout never lowers stage, so stage=4 and opted_out=true coexist, enabling t16.
